communicative_purpose: Polite way to express intent to purchase something
trigger_situations: Entering a shop, approaching a salesperson, beginning a purchase
contexts: Clothing stores, electronics shops, any retail environment
variations: je voudrais acheter, j'aimerais acheter, je veux acheter (informal)
common_mistakes: Using 'acheter' without 'voudrais' makes it too direct; forgetting the infinitive form
collocates: je voudrais acheter une robe, je voudrais acheter ceci, je voudrais acheter deux
example_2: 'je voudrais acheter' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'I would like to buy' is a useful expression.
example_3_translation: I know I would like to buy.

communicative_purpose: Asking the price of an item when shopping
trigger_situations: Seeing something interesting, comparing prices, before purchasing
contexts: Markets, shops, boutiques, any commercial transaction
variations: c'est combien, ça coûte combien, c'est à combien
common_mistakes: Forgetting 'c'est' and saying 'est combien' (incorrect); confusing with 'combien de temps'
collocates: c'est combien, c'est combien ça, c'est combien le kilo
example_2_translation: 'how much is it' is a useful expression.
example_3_translation: I know how much it is.

communicative_purpose: Asking if a specific clothing size is available
trigger_situations: Trying clothes, finding your size unavailable, checking stock
contexts: Clothing stores, shoe shops, any apparel retailer
variations: vous avez cette taille, est-ce que vous avez la taille, vous l'avez en taille
common_mistakes: Forgetting the article 'la'; using masculine 'le' instead of 'la' for clothing
collocates: vous avez la taille moyenne, vous avez cette taille, vous avez la taille quarante
example_2: 'vous avez la taille' est une expression utile.
example_3: Je connais vous avez la taille.
example_2_translation: 'you have the size' is a useful expression.
example_3_translation: I know you have the size.

communicative_purpose: Specifying color and product when shopping or describing clothing
trigger_situations: Pointing at items, making requests, describing what you want
contexts: Clothing stores, boutiques, when describing outfits
variations: la robe noire, un pulls noir, une jupe noire
common_mistakes: Putting adjective before noun (most French adjectives come after); color agreement with gender
collocates: une robe noire, un pantalon bleu, une chemise blanche, des chaussures rouges
example_2: 'une robe noire' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'a black dress' is a useful expression.

communicative_purpose: Stating your clothing size when shopping
trigger_situations: When asked for size, trying clothes on, shopping for yourself
contexts: Clothing stores, when shopping for clothes, online shopping
variations: je fais medium, je suis taille moyenne, je prends du M
common_mistakes: Using English size names without context; confusing French sizing system
collocates: je fais medium, je fais du quarante, je fais une taille moyenne
example_2: 'je fais medium' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'I do medium' is a useful expression.

communicative_purpose: Asking if card payment is available
trigger_situations: At checkout, when you have no cash, before paying
contexts: Shops, restaurants, any retail or service establishment
variations: acceptez-vous les cartes, vous prenez les cartes, peut-on payer par carte
common_mistakes: Forgetting the 's' on 'acceptez' (formal); using 'cartes' without article in singular
collocates: acceptez-vous les cartes, les cartes de crédit, vous prenez la carte
example_2: 'accepté-vous les cartes' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'you accepted the cards' is a useful phrase.
example_3_translation: I know you accepted the cards.

communicative_purpose: Asking permission to pay or announcing intention to pay
trigger_situations: At checkout, ready to pay, finishing shopping
variations: je peux payer, puis-je payer, je voudrais payer
common_mistakes: Using 'je peux' as a question without rising intonation or inversion; confusing with 'je veux payer'
collocates: je peux payer, je peux payer par carte, je peux payer en espèces
example_2: 'je peux payer' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'I can pay' is a useful expression.

communicative_purpose: Expressing that an item costs more than you're willing to pay
trigger_situations: When price is too high, negotiating, deciding not to buy
contexts: Markets, shops, bargaining situations, everyday shopping
variations: c'est trop cher, c'est trop coûteux, c'est hors de prix
common_mistakes: Saying 'c'est trop coûteux' (less common); not qualifying HOW expensive
collocates: c'est trop cher, c'est un peu cher, c'est vraiment cher
example_2: 'c'est trop cher' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'it's too expensive' is a useful expression.

meaning: do you have this in blue
communicative_purpose: Asking if an item is available in a different color
trigger_situations: Liking an item but wanting a different color, checking stock
contexts: Clothing stores, shoe shops, home goods stores
variations: vous avez ceci en bleu, est-ce que vous l'avez en blanc, vous avez ce modèle en noir
common_mistakes: Forgetting 'en' before color; using wrong preposition with colors
collocates: vous avez ceci en bleu, vous avez cette robe en rouge, vous l'avez en vert
example_2: 'vous avez ceci en bleu' est une expression utile.
example_3: Je connais vous avez ceci en bleu.
example_2_translation: 'you have this in blue' is a useful phrase.
example_3_translation: I know you have this in blue.

communicative_purpose: Indicating you need help finding something without being specific
trigger_situations: Entering a store, needing help, browsing for gifts
contexts: Any retail store, shopping centers, markets
variations: je cherche quelque chose, je cherche quelque chose de special, je cherche
common_mistakes: Adding unnecessary articles after 'quelque chose'; confusing with 'je voudrais quelque chose'
collocates: je cherche quelque chose, je cherche un cadeau, je cherche quelque chose pour ma mère
example_2: 'je cherche quelque chose' est une expression utile.
example_3: Je connais je cherche quelque chose.
example_2_translation: 'I'm looking for something' is a useful expression.
example_3_translation: I know I'm looking for something.

communicative_purpose: Referring to the middle size category when shopping
trigger_situations: When medium size is needed, checking availability, trying clothes
contexts: Clothing stores, when describing sizes, online shopping filters
variations: la taille moyenne, moyen, du moyen, une taille intermédiaire
common_mistakes: Confusing masculine/feminine forms (moyen vs moyenne); mixing up size terminology
collocates: la taille moyenne, taille petite, taille grande, vous avez la taille moyenne
example_2: 'la taille moyenne' est une expression utile.

communicative_purpose: Indicating an item is discounted or asking if something is on sale
trigger_situations: Seeing sale signs, asking about prices, shopping during sales periods
contexts: Department stores, boutiques, end of season sales
variations: c'est en solde, c'est réductions, il y a une promo, c'est discounté
common_mistakes: Confusing 'solde' (sale) with 'soldes' (plural, more common); using English 'sale'
collocates: c'est en solde, c'est pas en solde, il y a des soldes
example_2: 'c'est en solde' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'It's on sale' is a useful phrase.

meaning: I would like to try on
communicative_purpose: Asking to try on clothes before buying
trigger_situations: Finding clothes you like, checking fit, before purchasing
contexts: Clothing stores, shoe shops, fitting rooms
variations: je voudrais essayer, j'aimerais essayer, je peux essayer
common_mistakes: Forgetting that 'essayer' requires 'de' before another verb (essayer de faire); confusing with English structure
collocates: je voudrais essayer, je voudrais essayer cette robe, où sont les essayages
example_2: 'je voudrais essayer' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'I would like to try' is a useful expression.
example_3_translation: I know I would like to try.

communicative_purpose: Direct question about the cost of an item
trigger_situations: Before buying, comparing prices, discussing costs
contexts: Shops, markets, restaurants, any commercial context
variations: combien ça coûte, c'est quoi le prix, quel est le prix
common_mistakes: Forgetting that 'coûte' must agree with plural subjects; confusing with 'c'est combien'
collocates: combien ça coûte, combien coûtent ces chaussures, ça coûte combien
example_2: 'combien ça coûte' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'how much does it cost' is a useful phrase.
example_3_translation: I know how much it costs.

communicative_purpose: Specifying a clothing item with its color
trigger_situations: Shopping for clothes, describing what you want, pointing at items
contexts: Clothing stores, boutiques, when describing outfits
variations: la veste rouge, un blouson rouge, cette veste-ci rouge
common_mistakes: Color agreement errors (rouge vs rougeS for masculine plural); adjective position
collocates: une veste rouge, un manteau noir, une jupe bleue, des baskets blanches
example_2: 'une veste rouge' est une expression utile.
example_2_translation: 'a red jacket' is a useful expression.
